mechanism: Inhibits FLT3 and multiple protein kinases, blocking proliferative signaling in AML cells
additional_notes: Also known as PKC412; approved for FLT3‑mutated AML; MOLM‑13 is FLT3‑ITD–positive and highly sensitive; suggested IC50 ~5–50 nM

mechanism: Selective FLT3 tyrosine kinase inhibitor that blocks FLT3‑driven signaling and proliferation
additional_notes: Also known as ASP2215; approved for relapsed/refractory FLT3‑mutated AML; strong activity in MOLM‑13

mechanism: Potent FLT3‑ITD tyrosine kinase inhibitor reducing downstream survival and proliferation signals
additional_notes: Also known as AC220; highly active in FLT3‑ITD–positive AML including MOLM‑13; IC50 often <10 nM

mechanism: Inhibits multiple kinases such as RAF, VEGFR, PDGFR, and FLT3, impairing proliferative and survival pathways
additional_notes: Approved for solid tumors; used off‑label in FLT3‑ITD AML; MOLM‑13 IC50 typically ~50–300 nM

mechanism: Inhibits BCR‑ABL, SRC family kinases and other tyrosine kinases, disrupting oncogenic signaling
additional_notes: Approved for CML/ALL; reported to inhibit proliferation and signaling in some AML lines

mechanism: Inhibits BCR‑ABL and KIT tyrosine kinases, reducing downstream proliferative signaling
additional_notes: CML drug with additional activity against KIT/FLT3; some AML cell data, especially KIT/FLT3‑dependent

mechanism: Broad‑spectrum tyrosine kinase inhibitor targeting BCR‑ABL, FLT3 and others to block leukemic signaling
additional_notes: Shown to kill FLT3‑ITD AML cells including MOLM‑13; expected IC50 ~5–50 nM

mechanism: Selective inhibition of anti‑apoptotic protein BCL‑2, promoting mitochondrial apoptosis in AML cells
additional_notes: Also known as ABT‑199; approved with hypomethylating agents for AML; MOLM‑13 is BCL‑2–dependent and very sensitive; IC50 commonly 1–20 nM

mechanism: Inhibits anti‑apoptotic proteins BCL‑2 and BCL‑xL to trigger apoptosis
additional_notes: Also known as ABT‑263; clinical‑stage drug with strong apoptosis induction in many AML lines

mechanism: Inhibits class I/II histone deacetylases, leading to histone hyperacetylation, altered gene expression and apoptosis
additional_notes: Also known as LBH589; approved for multiple myeloma; numerous reports of potent anti‑AML activity; AML IC50s often ~5–50 nM

mechanism: Inhibits histone deacetylases, causing chromatin relaxation and modulation of genes controlling cell cycle and apoptosis
additional_notes: Also known as SAHA; approved for cutaneous T‑cell lymphoma (CTCL); widely used in AML preclinical studies; typical AML IC50 0.5–5 µM

mechanism: Incorporates into DNA and inhibits DNA methyltransferases, leading to DNA hypomethylation and re‑expression of silenced genes
additional_notes: Approved for AML/MDS; functional cytotoxic IC50 in vitro higher than incorporation levels; many AML lines show IC50 ~0.1–2 µM; unstable—refresh if >48 h

mechanism: Incorporates into DNA and RNA, inhibiting DNA methyltransferases and altering RNA function to induce hypomethylation and cytotoxicity
additional_notes: Approved for AML/MDS; cytotoxic in AML lines at low‑µM concentrations; IC50 usually ~1–10 µM; unstable—refresh daily

mechanism: Inhibits JAK1 and JAK2 kinases, blocking cytokine‑driven JAK/STAT signaling
additional_notes: Approved for myelofibrosis; some AML with activated cytokine/JAK signaling show sensitivity; AML IC50 commonly 0.5–5 µM

mechanism: Selective inhibition of PI3Kδ, reducing PI3K/AKT signaling important for survival and proliferation
additional_notes: Approved for CLL and some lymphomas; PI3K/AKT pathway is active in AML; some AML cell data exist; expected IC50 mid‑nM to low‑µM if sensitive

mechanism: Inhibits mTORC1, leading to reduced protein synthesis and cell growth
additional_notes: Also known as RAD001; approved for various solid tumors; mTOR inhibition can suppress AML proliferation; AML IC50s often ~10–500 nM

mechanism: Forms a complex with FKBP12 to inhibit mTORC1, decreasing cell growth and proliferation
additional_notes: Also known as Rapamycin; immunosuppressant with extensive AML cell data; typical AML IC50 ~1–100 nM

category: AMPK activator / mitochondrial complex I inhibitor
mechanism: Inhibits mitochondrial complex I and activates AMPK, leading to altered cellular metabolism and reduced proliferation
additional_notes: Widely used antidiabetic; anti‑proliferative effects in AML and other cancers typically at high‑µM to mM; AML lines often require 1–10 mM

mechanism: Inhibits aldehyde dehydrogenase and, often in the presence of copper, interferes with proteasome and redox pathways to induce cell death
additional_notes: Alcohol‑aversion drug; reported to kill AML stem/progenitor cells, especially with Cu2+; IC50 in many cancer lines ~0.1–2 µM; protect from light

mechanism: Acts as a mitochondrial uncoupler and modulates pathways such as Wnt and STAT3, disrupting energy metabolism and signaling
additional_notes: Anthelmintic; preclinical AML activity via Wnt/STAT3 inhibition and mitochondrial disruption; low aqueous solubility—ensure good mixing; AML IC50 often ~0.2–2 µM
